source_jobs.title: Senior Enterprise Customer Success Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Accord http://inaccord.com/ is a breakout Series A startup (funding announcement https://techcrunch.com/2023/01/24/accord-which-offers-a-platform-to-manage-sales-processes-secures-10m/) founded by proven leaders from Stripe, LinkedIn, and Google. Accord’s high-bar for execution has quickly led to big wins, including:

 - Driving 100+ paying customers under 12 months of launching

 - Partnering with industry leaders such as Samsara, http://harvey.ai/Harvey.ai http://Harvey.ai, Decagon, Xactly etc.

 - Raving reviews from it’s 10,000+ users, G2 Feedback https://www.g2.com/products/accord-accord/reviews

The company is on a mission to transform how Revenue Leaders drive Execution Excellence. From setting and enforcing standards for how their GTM team sells, onboards, and expands with customers, ambitious leaders use Accord to up-level the craft of Sales.

With hubs in Toronto & San Francisco, Accord is bringing on passionate builders who sweat the details and want to build something special.

If you’re interested in being part of a collaborative, high-velocity team that is dedicated to excellence in its craft, apply.



THE ROLE



We're looking for a Senior Enterprise Customer Success Manager to own the full customer lifecycle for our most strategic accounts, including: onboarding, implementation, adoption, expansion, and renewal.



Accord helps revenue organizations transform how they execute the customer journey. This role is a true partner to revenue leadership at our customers: CROs, VPs of Sales, RevOps, and Sales Enablement.



In this key role, you will define how this function operates as much as you execute it. You'll personally lead 8-12+ week transformational implementations and advise a portfolio of 10-15 matrixed enterprise accounts, with 35-50+ active stakeholders per account spanning rev ops, sales leadership, enablement, and frontline reps.



If you thrive on ownership, love building deep relationships with revenue leaders, and want direct influence on both your customers’ success and Accord’s product and GTM strategy, we'd love to meet you.




WHAT YOU'LL DO


 - Own the full customer journey: Lead onboarding, hands-on implementation (typically 8-12+ weeks of change management), and adoption for enterprise customers from kickoff to go-live.

 - Multi-thread complex organizations: Build and maintain relationships across 35-50+ stakeholders per account, executive sponsors, champions, technical evaluators, and end users.

 - Drive measurable outcomes: Translate Accord adoption into quantified revenue impact, shorter sales cycle, higher win rates, methodology adherence, rep ramp time, etc.

 - Own renewals and expansion: Lead renewal forecasting, negotiation, and close. Identify and sequence expansion opportunities across our product suite.

 - Be a strategic advisor: Coach revenue leaders through process change, methodology adoption, and organizational alignment.

 - Build the foundation: Contribute to scalable processes, playbooks, and customer education as we grow the CS function. Bring structure to ambiguity.

 - Partner cross-functionally: Collaborate with Sales, Product, and Marketing and provide feedback that informs product roadmap and GTM strategy.

 - Champion our customers internally: Represent customer needs directly to founders, product leadership, shaping our roadmap and GTM strategy.
   
   


WHO YOU ARE


 - Experienced operator: 5+ years as an operator in Customer Success, Account Management, or Implementation at a B2B SaaS company, managing mid-market and Enterprise accounts. Experience in sales tools/technology, or working closely with sales leaders, is a plus.

 - Strategic Advisor: Senior-level presence with proven influence at the C-suite on customer accounts. Rigorous and structured in communication. You know what each stakeholder values, you can map who actually makes purchasing decisions in a complex buying committee, and you can hold your own with a CRO.

 - Implementation dep

source_jobs.title: Head of Growth
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Accord http://inaccord.com/ is a breakout Series A startup (funding announcement https://techcrunch.com/2023/01/24/accord-which-offers-a-platform-to-manage-sales-processes-secures-10m/) founded by proven leaders from Stripe, LinkedIn, and Google. Accord’s high-bar for execution has quickly led to big wins, including:

 - Driving 100+ paying customers under 12 months of launching

 - Partnering with industry leaders such as Samsara, http://harvey.ai/Harvey.ai http://Harvey.ai, Decagon, Xactly etc.

 - Raving reviews from it’s 10,000+ users, G2 Feedback https://www.g2.com/products/accord-accord/reviews

The company is on a mission to transform how Revenue Leaders drive Execution Excellence. From setting and enforcing standards for how their GTM team sells, onboards, and expands with customers, ambitious leaders use Accord to up-level the craft of Sales.

With hubs in Toronto & San Francisco, Accord is bringing on passionate builders who sweat the details and want to build something special.

If you’re interested in being part of a collaborative, high-velocity team that is dedicated to excellence in its craft, apply.



THE ROLE

We’re looking for a sharp, early-stage growth leader to join Accord and add firepower to our pipeline engine as our Head of Growth. You’ll report directly to our VP of Marketing, Lenny, and own the strategy for driving high-intent demos and a multi-six-figure enterprise pipeline. You likely bring a few years of learnings and failures from scaling a startup, having managed six-figure budgets and navigated the $100k+ enterprise deals we’re tackling here.



This isn’t a brand awareness role, it is a high-accountability growth position. You will sit at the intersection of pipeline strategy and performance moving beyond traditional paid ads to pioneer AI Engine Optimization (AEO). Your goal is to make sure that when anyone asks an LLM or a community like Reddit about sales excellence, Accord is the first name they see. You’ll join a fast-moving team to bridge the gap between Product and Sales, refining our unit economics as we scale.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Pipeline: Own the primary goal of meeting bookings and pipeline generation, specifically targeting high-value enterprise deals ($100k+).

 - Multi-Channel Demand: Strategize and execute paid media across LinkedIn, Google, YouTube, and the Meta suite to create and capture demand.

 - AEO & Content Growth: Lead our AI Engine Optimization strategy. You’ll leverage written content and community citations (Reddit, etc.) to ensure Accord is the top-of-mind solution within ChatGPT, Claude, and other LLMs.

 - Integrated ABM Campaigns: Partner with Product Marketing and Content to translate value props into high-converting campaigns and virtual events.

 - Full-Funnel Execution: Manage the demand function with a sharp eye on unit economics, specifically working to refine and optimize

 - Agile Experimentation: Continuously A/B test creative ad formats and innovative campaigns that break through the noise of typical B2B SaaS
   


WHO YOU ARE

 - Experience: 5+ years in Demand Gen or Growth roles, with a proven track record of owning the function end-to-end in a B2B SaaS environment.

 - Enterprise Scale: You’ve marketed to enterprise organizations (2000+ employees) and understand the complexities in navigating a complex, multi-stakeholder buying process for multi-six-figure deals

 - ABM Expertise: You have a proven track record of running account-based marketing that results in meetings, not just impressions.

 - Proven Results: You have personally owned and driven a pipeline in excess of $10M in previous roles.

 - The Tech Stack: High proficiency in Google Analytics, Microsoft Clarity, and major ad platforms (LinkedIn, Google, YouTube). Experience with HubSpot is a strong plus.

 - AI-Native Workflow: You don’t just use AI; it’s at the forefront of how you research, iterate, and move faster than the competition.

 - The "AEO" Mindset: You understand t

source_jobs.title: Field Marketing Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Accord http://inaccord.com/ is a breakout Series A startup (funding announcement https://techcrunch.com/2023/01/24/accord-which-offers-a-platform-to-manage-sales-processes-secures-10m/) founded by proven leaders from Stripe, LinkedIn, and Google. Accord’s high-bar for execution has quickly led to big wins, including:

 - Driving 100+ paying customers under 12 months of launching

 - Partnering with industry leaders such as Samsara, http://harvey.ai/Harvey.ai http://Harvey.ai, Decagon, Xactly etc.

 - Raving reviews from it’s 10,000+ users, G2 Feedback https://www.g2.com/products/accord-accord/reviews

The company is on a mission to transform how Revenue Leaders drive Execution Excellence. From setting and enforcing standards for how their GTM team sells, onboards, and expands with customers, ambitious leaders use Accord to up-level the craft of Sales.

With hubs in Toronto & San Francisco, Accord is bringing on passionate builders who sweat the details and want to build something special.

If you’re interested in being part of a collaborative, high-velocity team that is dedicated to excellence in its craft, apply.




THE ROLE



As Accord’s Field Marketing Manager, you will be responsible for the end-to-end management of our in-person event calendar - from intimate executive dinners to industry-leading revenue conferences. The role has a strong focus on intimate, high-impact experiences for revenue leaders, including multi-day summits at premier destinations. Reporting directly to Lenny, VP, Marketing https://www.linkedin.com/in/lennyohm/, you’ll collaborate closely with your marketing peers in product marketing, content and demand gen as well as sales to bring events to life and leave a lasting impression with top revenue leaders to drive pipeline growth.




YOU WILL


 - Execute & define a field event plan. You will own the full event lifecycle, pre- and post-event coordination, logistics, timelines, and deliverables, while managing a full calendar of 5-7 events at any given time.

 - Own our flagship User Summit end-to-end. This is the highlight of the events calendar; you will lead planning and execution in unique, standout locations that create a memorable experience for attendees.

 - Design and deliver a variety of field events. From intimate executive dinners and sports suites to sponsored activations, including 3-4 marquee events per year at destinations like Deer Valley and Palisades Tahoe for top revenue leaders.

 - Represent Accord at major industry conferences. You will manage Accord’s presence at the large-scale conferences we attend.

 - Build integrated event campaigns. Partner with Demand Generation, Product Marketing, Content, and Sales to maximize engagement before, during, and after every event.

 - Partner closely with AEs and CSMs to ensure key customers & prospects are included in the appropriate events.

 - Manage all event-related tasks, including securing invites, gifting, booth logistics, sponsorship deliverables, budget tracking, and coordination of attending staff.

 - Support the GTM team attending events by providing clear expectations and timelines, and ensuring all event requirements are delivered.

 - Coordinate with marketing to ensure events are aligned with goals and messaging, maximizing attendance, engagement, and results.

 - Track and analyze key metrics (ROI, meetings booked, and deals created), providing detailed results to demonstrate event effectiveness and inform future strategy.

 - Track expenses and ensure all events are delivered on time and within budget.

 - Bring creative ideas to life. You will have the opportunity to shape the creative vision for events from concept through execution, ensuring a consistent and compelling brand presence that differentiates Accord.

 - Travel as needed to support execution and on-the-ground logistics (estimated 2-3 trips per quarter).
   
   


WHO YOU ARE


 - 3+ years of proven experience in B2B events planning and management.

notes: All job postings are sales/GTM roles, no tech roles found.
